title: How to Win at the Sport of Business

chapters.title: Explore your options before you start
chapters.text:
The knowledge that Cuban got from magazines, books, and observing up-and-coming technology companies was immeasurable.

Watching the best taught me how to run my businesses. Along the way, I taught myself a few things. ~ Mark Cuban

These insights helped him understand the reasons behind shifts in hard drives and computer prices, keeping him in business. His partnership with Michael Dell of Dell C omputers proved to be life-changing. Additionally, by learning from Bill Gates' decisions and strategies, he built the habit of thinking ahead of the competition.

Making a plan and setting rules are the first steps to running a successful business.

But unlike sports, where you know your competitors and other important information that could positively influence your winning, business requires much more effort.

You can gain an advantage if you realize that business happens every day. As long as you stay focused, you can overcome every challenge to success.

In the game of business, proficiency is rare. Those who excel have what it takes to execute plans and know exactly what to do at all times.

Unlike sports, there are no clear and well-known rules in business. But you can develop your own principles to guide you through times of uncertainty.

Did you know? A World Bank report states that Small and Medium Enterprises are the foundation of the global economy. SMEs form 90% of all businesses worldwide and provide 50% of all jobs.

chapters.title: Cultivate the courage to succeed in business
chapters.text:
Getting a job or running a business can be tricky, especially when you have several doubts. But bills won't wait for you to be satisfied with your success. They will keep piling up.

Everyone's situation is unique and requires a customized approach. Fresh out of university, Mark Cuban began trying out other jobs. He didn't find his true passion for a while, and getting the position he wanted was almost impossible due to his lack of qualifications. After several attempts, he got a job at the first software store in Dallas.

Over time, he built a client base, rendering extra software-related services to people .

When the company fired Cuban nine months later, he started selling personal computers and software, as well as helping companies and customers set them up. After much deliberation, he came up with a company called MicroSolutions, Inc. Cuban even received two initial offers from his previous employers.

People trusted him with jobs not because he was the most qualified but because he did whatever it took to get the job done.

Get a broad business education rather than majoring in just one marketing field.

Most people do not devote extra time to learning new skills and solely focus on delivering their best in assigned projects, giving you an edge over them.

chapters.title: Learn new things to stay one step ahead of the competition
chapters.text:
The knowledge that Cuban got from magazines, books, and observing up-and-coming technology companies was immeasurable.

Watching the best taught me how to run my businesses. Along the way, I taught myself a few things. ~ Mark Cuban

His partnership with Michael Dell of Dell C omputers proved to be life-changing. Additionally, by learning from Bill Gates' decisions and strategies, he built the habit of thinking ahead of the competition.

Making a plan and setting rules are the first steps to running a successful business.

But unlike sports, where you know your competitors and other important information that could positively influence your winning, business requires much more effort.

You can gain an advantage if you realize that business happens every day. As long as you stay focused, you can overcome every challenge to success.

In the game of business, proficiency is rare. Those who excel have what it takes to execute plans and know exactly what to do at all times.

Unlike sports, there are no clear and well-known rules in business. But you can develop your own principles to guide you through times of uncertainty.

Did you know? A World Bank report states that Small and Medium Enterprises are the foundation of the global economy. SMEs form 90% of all businesses worldwide and provide 50% of all jobs.

chapters.title: Explore your options before you start
chapters.text:
At 27, with an income of $60,000 that year, Cuban knew he had to do something to improve his business since the PC industry had experienced some setbacks. But he understood that the only thing an entrepreneur or athlete can control is how much effort they put in. It is always your responsibility to take conscious steps that will guarantee success for you and your business.

But you don't need to have it all figured out at an early age. Spend these years trying out various pursuits until you find something you love. Once you know this, you can focus.

Figuring out what works for you will help you achieve your goals faster.

Your failures are necessary for growth. Each failure is a reminder that something better is possible .

While the initial stages of business may evoke overwhelming feelings of fear, hope, and excitement, these rules can help put things into perspective :

• Honestly assess yourself and recognize your wins and losses. Focus on doing what brings the most money.
• Build a trustworthy team and delegate responsibilities to them.
• Avoid being overwhelmed by numerous opportunities; prioritize core values and needs before exploring additional options.

In business, it doesn't matter how many times you fail. You may have to try dozens of times before you make the right move that transforms your life.

Many people don't care about your past struggles, how you started from a rough patch and failed through a lot. They see your results, and that's what intrigues them.

chapters.title: Be realistic about your capabilities
chapters.text:
As an entrepreneur, it's tempting to exaggerate strengths and downplay weaknesses. You may have doubts that some things are beyond your capabilities if you believe that, with maximum effort, you can solve all your problems. But this isn't always the case.

The worst evaluator of talent is a player trying to evaluate himself. ~ Don Nelson

It is always up to you to choose between reality and your ideal self. Acknowledging that you're not a superhero opens the door to finding other ways to accomplish tasks.

It's easy to believe that a lot of money is needed to get work done, but that's rarely the case in the long run. It's okay to start small. It is not a good idea to rely on investors for initial capital since they are only concerned about gaining a return on their investment. Instead, your own money and that of your customers are the most reliable sources of income for a startup business.

Learning does not have to be expensive to be relevant to your growth.

Businesses that become successful usually start small because you don't need more cash; you need more effort. To truly connect with your client, you must look at things from your client's angle. Try to experience your company as someone else without any false impressions would. Attend your events, buy your products, discover the mistakes, and correct them. While no company is flawless, a CEO's detachment from customers raises red flags.

chapters.title: You can't please everyone, and that's okay
chapters.text:
Sometimes, you have to take “no” for an answer. It allows you to learn why your customers are saying no and to improve your services. If you've addressed their objections and still face resistance, move on; not everyone will be your customer.

Be confident in your business; it will help you inspire current and potential customers.

You waste everyone's time when you push your products on people who aren't interested. Additionally, it makes you look desperate, which is a sign of laziness since finding new clients requires effort. Instead, believe in your business. Acknowledge that rejection is part of your journey, and redirect your focus toward more promising prospects.

It isn't your customers' job to know what you don't. Your job is to improve things for the better by trying to invent the best possible version of your product. Your customers will tell you what they want, so listen to them, but do not rely on them for a blueprint of your business. That's your responsibility.

Venture outside your comfort zone to find better opportunities for your business, even if it means going where few people go. You might encounter failure, but no matter what happens, be determined to enjoy everything you do.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
The b usiness world is anything but a bed of roses. You might get tired from time to time or even feel like quitting. But as Mark Cuban explained, you can be successful if you put in enough effort. Through hard work and a strong focus on your goals, you can be the best in whatever field you choose.

The trick to making it big as an entrepreneur is to follow the path of least resistance. Many people take the easy way in life, seeking less stress — a principle embodied by giants like Amazon.com, Apple, and Google, which has made them successful. Google streamlines keyword searches, while Amazon offers hassle-free home shopping and doorstep delivery .

Additionally, your business will not pause for you to get over your losses or problems. The game continues until you call it quits, so don't give up without a fight.

Remember, the world is your arena; all you have to do is be ready to play the sport of business.

Try this


• Keep up with industry trends and learn from both successes and failures.
• Be open to change and willing to pivot your strategies when necessary .
• Assess your strengths and weaknesses honestly.
• Remember that a successful business often starts small and grows through consistent effort.
• Listen to your clients and innovate based on their needs.
